title: Goods Movement
learning_objectives: Understand Goods Movement in SAP PP.
Learn different movement types used during production.
Understand Goods Issue and Goods Receipt.
Learn standard SAP Goods Movement Transaction Codes.

introduction.title: What is Goods Movement?
introduction.content: Goods Movement is the process of recording the physical movement of materials in SAP. During production, raw materials are issued from inventory to the Production Order, and after manufacturing, finished goods are received back into inventory. Every Goods Movement automatically updates stock quantities and creates accounting documents.

business_purpose.title: Why is Goods Movement Important?
business_purpose.content: Goods Movement ensures inventory accuracy, records material consumption, updates production costs and maintains traceability of materials throughout the manufacturing process.

goods_movement_types.description: Raw materials are issued from inventory to the Production Order.
Finished goods are received into inventory after production.
Material is transferred between storage locations or plants.
Corrects inventory differences identified during stock verification.

integration.purpose: Records component consumption and finished goods.
Goods Receipt may trigger quality inspection.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A Production Order requires stainless steel scrap, ferro alloys and fluxes. Goods Issue (Movement Type 261) posts these materials to production. After manufacturing is completed, 1,000 stainless steel coils are received into inventory using Goods Receipt (Movement Type 101), updating warehouse stock and financial records.

best_practices: Reconcile physical stock with SAP inventory regularly.
important_notes: Every Goods Movement creates a Material Document.
Most Goods Movements also generate Accounting Documents.
Movement Type determines how SAP updates inventory.
Goods Issue should normally occur before Production Confirmation is finalized.
jsl_notes: Production teams should verify material availability before posting Goods Issue.
Goods Receipt should be posted only after production and quality verification, as per plant procedures.
Monitor failed Goods Movements using COGI and resolve errors promptly.
